summary: iOS | Morpheus | Unable to track inCall status
scenario: iOS app fails to detect active call state on Voyager 4245 Office (Morpheus) devices, preventing call-aware alerts and blocking of DFU/ANC changes
description_clean: The iOS app cannot reliably detect when a user is in an active call when connected to Morpheus (Voyager 4245 Office) devices. This prevents the app from showing appropriate alerts and from blocking DFU (firmware updates) or ANC/transparency mode changes during a call. The fix involves using deckardId 0x0E22 instead of 0x0E35 to correctly read call state for these devices.
steps: 1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app on iOS device.
2. Connect to a Morpheus (Voyager 4245 Office) device via Bluetooth.
3. Initiate or receive an active phone call on the iOS device.
4. While in the active call, observe the Poly Studio Mobile app Home screen.
5. Attempt to trigger a DFU (firmware update) from the app.
6. Attempt to change ANC or Transparency Mode settings from the app (Device Settings → Audio Settings → ANC/Transparency Mode).
expected: The app should detect the active call state, display appropriate call-related alerts, and block DFU firmware updates and ANC/Transparency Mode changes during the call.
actual: The app does not reliably detect the active call state. It fails to show appropriate alerts and does not block DFU firmware updates or ANC/Transparency Mode changes during the call.
